authority: bounded survey-state reconciliation only; not whole-demand-model closure, ecosystem completeness, adoption, execution, admission, authoring, Hook, runtime, cross-repository, standards, commit, or push authority

sourcePins.authority: read-only candidate, evidence, and research input; not Skill, Hook, product, admission, or standards authority

coordinateEnvelope.interpretation: Every coordinate in the bounded 62-row STM/P/SG envelope has at least one source-supported demand lane, a disposition, a verification method, limitations, and a recheck trigger. This does not make the demand model exhaustive.

gapClasses.effect: No external or repository-authored Skill, Hook, or additional structure is justified by the current bounded evidence.
Bind a concrete runtime use case and collect live evidence before inferring a capability gap.

items: maintainer adaptation over repeated feedback cycles
gapClasses.effect: Keep these as research and feedback questions rather than converting them into mandatory controls.
Wait for repeated cross-source, cross-host, cross-task evidence and a separate authority decision.

decision.nextGate: Resume bounded discovery or comparison only after a new source-supported demand, repeated failure, relevant source or license drift, host or runtime change, material cost change, or authority-boundary change.

recheckTriggers: a new source-supported demand selects a materially different problem or subject boundary
a repeated native, current-chain, curated, recipe, runtime, fallback, or human-authority path failure is reproduced
a reviewed source, license, dependency, executable surface, or exact revision changes materially
an Agent, model, loader, permission, workspace, Hook, Plugin, App, MCP, or runtime capability changes the comparison
behavioral, longitudinal, production, cross-host, cost, or counterevidence changes a disposition
admission, repository authoring, Hook, standardization, phase-close, commit, push, or cross-repository delivery is proposed
nonAuthorization: no candidate execution, installation, adaptation, vendoring, admission, release, or runtime activation
no live Agent Home, Hook, Plugin, App, MCP, account, or user-configuration mutation
no cross-repository write, commit, or push
